0.text: ARB 027/2024 Nalani v Netty OCTOBER 02, 2025 ARBITRATION - ORDERS

1.text: IN THE DUBAI INTERNATIONAL FINANCIAL CENTRE COURTS

2.text: IN THE COURT OF FIRST INSTANCE

9.text: ORDER WITH REASONS OF H.E. JUSTICE SHAMLAN AL SAWALEHI

10.text: UPON the Order of H.E. Justice Shamlan Al Sawalehi dated 22 September 2025 dismissing the Defendant’s PTA Application and the Stay Application (the “Order”) (the “Applications”)

11.text: AND UPON paragraph 3 of the Order directing that the Defendant pay the Claimant’s costs of the Applications

12.text: AND UPON the Claimant’s Statement of Costs dated 10 September 2025

13.text: AND UPON reviewing the Statement of Costs and the supporting material

14.text: AND UPON considering Part 38 of the Rules of the DIFC Courts (“RDC”) and Practice Direction No. 4 of 2017 (Interest on Judgments)

16.text: 1. The Defendant shall pay the Claimant AED 208,955.80 (the “Costs Award”), representing $80 \%$ of the total amount claimed in the Statement of Costs.

18.text: 2. The Costs Award shall be paid within 14 days from the date of this Order, pursuant to RDC 38.40.

19.text: 3. In the event the Defendant fails to pay the Costs Award within 14 days of this Order, interest shall accrue at the rate of $9 \%$ per annum from the date of this Order until full payment is made, in accordance with Practice Direction No. 4 of 2017.

20.text:
Issued by:   
Delvin Sumo   
Assistant Registrar   
Date of issue: 2 October 2025   
At: 10am

22.text:
1. This Order concerns the Claimant’s entitlement to costs pursuant to paragraph 3 of the Order dated   
22 September 2025 by which I dismissed the Defendant’s PTA Application and Stay Application.   
2. The Claimant duly fi led its Statement of Costs on 10 September 2025, claiming a total of AED   
261,194.75.

23.text: 3. I am satisfi ed that the hourly rates charged fall within the market range permitted by Registrar’s Direction No. 1 of 2023. The structure and staffi ng of the Claimant’s legal team, and the steps taken in these proceedings, were proportionate to the complexity and procedural demands of the matter.

24.text: 4. However, in the exercise of my discretion under RDC 38.8 and RDC 38.23, and applying the standard basis of assessment, I consider that a reduction is warranted. I assess the Claimant’s recoverable costs at $80 \%$ of the amount claimed.

25.text: 5. In the circumstances, I consider that AED 208,955.80 represents a fair, proportionate, and reasonable quantifi cation of the Claimant’s recoverable costs, in accordance with the overriding objective and the principles of judicial economy.

26.text: 6. Accordingly, I award the Claimant costs in that amount, payable within 14 days, with interest accruing thereafter in accordance with the DIFC Courts’ standard practice.
